name: Intercultural Family Services, Incorporated
description_short: Non-profit human services agency in Greater Philadelphia offering culturally competent behavioral health, housing counseling, and family support programs since 1979.
description_long:
Intercultural Family Services, Inc. is an independent 501(c)(3) non-profit organization founded in 1979 that has spent 45 years serving families and individuals throughout the Greater Philadelphia region. The organization was established with a commitment to addressing the needs of children and families in the area through culturally relevant and community-centered approaches.

The organization provides a broad array of human services including behavioral health services, community well-being and prevention programs, housing counseling, parenting resources, and family-centered support. They operate specific initiatives such as Healthy Start Program, PeerTalks, and Work to Ride. Services are delivered by multicultural and multilingual staff trained in cultural competency, with an emphasis on meeting families where they are and providing support tailored to individual circumstances.

Intercultural distinguishes itself through its explicit commitment to cultural competency and family-centered service delivery across all programs. The organization emphasizes collaborative partnerships with public and private sectors, maintains diversity in hiring and service provision, and operates as an Equal Opportunity Employer and Service Provider. Their core values of effective communication, commitment, quality of services, and trust are embedded in operations.

As a non-profit relying on donations and partnerships, service availability and depth may depend on funding cycles. While the website documents extensive programming, specific eligibility requirements, wait times, and service capacity details are not provided online. Prospective clients should contact the organization directly via phone (215-386-1298) or email (info@ifsinc.org) to discuss specific needs and access to services.
pros: 45-year track record of serving Greater Philadelphia families with established credibility and institutional knowledge
Culturally competent and multilingual staff trained to serve diverse populations and communities
Comprehensive service array spanning behavioral health, housing counseling, prevention programs, and parenting resources
Non-profit model eliminates profit motives and focuses mission on family stabilization and community wellbeing
Explicitly stated Equal Opportunity service provider with anti-discrimination policies across protected categories
Free or low-cost services funded through donations and public/private partnerships rather than client fees
Documented client testimonials highlighting relationship-building and information access alongside services
cons: Limited geographic scope restricted to Greater Philadelphia region, unavailable for customers outside service area
Website lacks specific information on service eligibility requirements, wait times, or service capacity
No pricing transparency or documented fee schedules available online for budget planning
Website does not specify which services are completely free versus sliding-scale or fee-based
No online portal, application system, or appointment booking available; requires phone/email contact for access
best_for: Families in Greater Philadelphia seeking culturally appropriate behavioral health and mental health support
Individuals and families needing housing counseling or assistance with housing stability
Parents seeking parenting resources, family strengthening programs, and preventive community services
diagnosis: Best for families and individuals in the Greater Philadelphia area needing free or low-cost culturally competent behavioral health, housing counseling, or family support services. Primary caveat: limited geographic service area and lack of online service details; direct contact required to verify eligibility and access availability.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
